launch.title: Launch
launch.subtitle: Start a coding agent through the proxy — its session is captured automatically.
launch.mode_subscription_note: Uses the account you're already logged into (claude / codex login). Nothing global is touched.
launch.mode_key_note: The key stays in server memory only — the proxy injects it on forward, so the agent and disk never see it.
launch.started: Started in a new terminal. The session will appear in Sessions once it makes a request.
launch.run_yourself_note: Point your own client at the proxy and run it — HTTP-only (no hooks), but you control everything. In key mode your key stays in your own shell. Copy registers a session for the token.
launch.run_yourself_registered: Session #{{id}} registered — run the command, it'll appear in Sessions.
launch.client_args_note: Optional native flags passed straight to the client, e.g. --resume to continue your last conversation. See the client's own docs for the rest.
launch.full_capture_note: Runs via tracelab launch, which also injects hook capture. Use this if you want hook events, not just HTTP.
launch.disabled_note: Server launch is off. Copy the command above, or restart serve with -allow-launch.

desktop.warning: This edits your global ~/.codex/config.toml (it can't be routed any other way). The original is backed up first and restored when you're done.
desktop.intro: Inject proxy routing into ~/.codex/config.toml, then start the Codex desktop app. Subscription only — your ~/.codex/auth.json is untouched.
desktop.hooks_note: Hooks are written too, but Codex gates them behind trust — approve them once via the app's /hooks. HTTP capture works regardless.
desktop.http_only_note: Only HTTP traffic will be captured — no hook events.
desktop.active: Routing is active. Original config backed up.
desktop.step_restart: Restart the Codex desktop app so it reloads the config.
desktop.step_trust: If prompted, trust tracelab's hooks once (app's /hooks).
desktop.step_done: When finished, click Stop & restore below.

subagent.title: Experiment 6 · Sub-agents
subagent.desc: A sub-agent's fork → run → report-a-summary-back is harness orchestration; replaying one request can't show it. You run it live with hooks on, and the flow shows SubagentStart/Stop bracketing the sub-agent's own requests.

s1.label: Launch cc with hooks on
s1.note: Hooks are the only way to see the sub-agent boundary — start a session from the Launch page; SubagentStart/Stop/Task* are on by default.

s2.label: Paste this into cc
s2.note: Self-contained — runs in any directory, needs no files or repo.
s2.prompt: Use the Task tool to launch a general-purpose sub-agent. Have it: (1) run `pwd` to see which directory it's in; (2) report back, in one line, what task it was given and whether it can see any of our earlier conversation. Don't do this yourself — delegate it.

s3.label: Back in tracelab, open this session's flow
s3.note: Look for PreToolUse(Task) → SubagentStart → the sub-agent's own requests → SubagentStop → the Task tool_result.

s4.label: Compare the sub-agent's request with the main agent's
s4.note: Different system prompt · trimmed tool set · fresh history · returns only a summary · same cwd.

compaction.title: Experiment 5 · Compaction
compaction.desc: The library has seed:cc-compaction (the summarize-trigger request only). The full story is PreCompact → last request before → PostCompact → first request after — across several requests + hooks. Run it to watch the context shrink.

s1.label: Launch cc with hooks on
s1.note: PreCompact / PostCompact mark the compaction boundary in the flow.

s2.label: Trigger compaction
s2.note: Run /compact for an instant manual trigger, or fill the context near the limit to trip auto-compaction.

s3.label: Open the flow and locate the boundary
s3.note: PreCompact → last request before → PostCompact → first request after.

s4.label: Compare the before/after requests, and open the compaction panel
s4.note: The summarize request's detail shows the input→summary token reduction; the next request starts from the summary instead of the full history.

compaction.title: Compaction
compaction.hint: A /compact request: the conversation so far was summarized. Below is how much context went in (incl. cache) vs the summary that came out.

cases.title: Replay library
cases.subtitle: Pick a case to run.

seed_desc.codex_pure_text: Experiment 1 — first request shape with a full tool catalog but a "just say hello" prompt.
seed_desc.codex_tool_read: Experiment 2 — how a tool_use + tool_result round-trip feeds back into the next request.
seed_desc.codex_apply_patch: Experiment 3 — codex's custom_tool_call (apply_patch) freeform grammar for editing files.
seed_desc.codex_tool_failure: Experiment 4 — how a non-zero exit / tool error is encoded back to the model.
seed_desc.cc_pure_text: Experiment 1 — first request shape with a full tool catalog but a "just say hello" prompt.
seed_desc.cc_tool_read: Experiment 2 — how a tool_use + tool_result round-trip feeds back into the next request.
seed_desc.cc_edit: Experiment 3 (cc) — editing a file via the structured Edit tool (old_string/new_string). cc has no apply_patch; this is its analog to codex's freeform apply_patch.
seed_desc.cc_tool_failure: Experiment 4 — how an is_error tool_result is encoded back to the model.
seed_desc.cc_title: Bonus — the short request Claude Code fires to auto-name a session.
seed_desc.cc_full_claude: Full request shape with cache_control breakpoints and adaptive thinking.
seed_desc.cc_compaction: Experiment 5 — a synthetic /compact trigger preserving the real wire shape: the model is asked to summarize the whole conversation into <analysis>/<summary>. Shows context_management (clear_thinking) + adaptive thinking.

cases.new_hint: Paste or edit request JSON. Runs will be routed through the selected session.
cases.name: Name
cases.warning: Running a case sends a real, billed request to upstream.
cases.needs_key_hint: This API-key session lost its key when tracelab restarted (keys are never written to disk). Re-enter it once — no need to restart the agent.
cases.needs_key_banner: This API-key session lost its key on restart (keys are never written to disk). Re-enter it to keep running — your still-open agent resumes on its next request, no relaunch needed.
cases.none_active: No live session in this process. Start one:
cases.close_session_confirm: Close this live session? It's dropped from the proxy (and its in-memory credentials are cleared), and replay can no longer use it. This does NOT stop the agent running in your terminal — but its next request will fail.
cases.overwrite_confirm: Overwrite this case in place? The original body is replaced — can't be undone.
cases.delete_confirm: Delete this case? This can't be undone.
cases.delete_seed_confirm: Delete this built-in case? It won't come back after a restart.
cases.curl_proxy_note: Hits tracelab's per-session proxy. Carries no secret — the proxy injects auth on forward. Works only while this session is alive.
cases.curl_direct_note: Hits upstream directly with this session's real credentials — portable, like Postman's copy-as-curl.
cases.curl_direct_warning: This command embeds real credentials. Anyone you paste it to (issue, screenshot, shared shell) can use them.
cases.curl_subscription_note: This is your login session token, not an API key you typed — and it can expire.

hooks.title: Hook capture
hooks.intro: Which lifecycle and tool events tracelab wires when it launches each agent. Seeded with the built-in defaults; add events a runtime ships before tracelab catches up, or disable noisy ones. Changes apply to the next launch — no restart.
hooks.add_hint: Unknown events are harmlessly ignored by the runtime, so adding one ahead of a release is safe.

sessions.title: Sessions
sessions.empty: No sessions captured yet. Launch cc or codex through the proxy.
sessions.delete_confirm: Delete this session and its {{count}} events? The raw bytes are removed too — can't be undone.
sessions.http_only_hint: Only HTTP was captured — no hook events. Launched via the env "run it yourself" path.

timeline.timeline_hint: Newest turns first; events inside each turn read top to bottom
timeline.flow_hint: Newest turns first; events inside each turn read top to bottom

flow.empty: No hook events in this turn
flow.hint: The harness hook timeline. Hooks tied to a request show a chip — open it for the LLM exchange.
flow.context_diff: Compare context vs the previous request

diff.no_prev: No earlier completion in this session to compare against.

replay.warning: Replay sends a real, billed request to the upstream API.
